original text:
This letter is being sent on behalf of Dr Conor Gilligan – a researcher from the University of Newcastle and Professor Dan Lubman and Ms Fiona Blee from Turning Point Alcohol and Drug Centre.
The researchers are interested in helping parents/guardians deal with the challenges associated with their children starting to drink alcohol and would like your help in finding the best way to work with parents.
You are invited to attend a workshop at the school at Collingwood College on Wednesday, 30 October at 6:30pm.
They will also be conducting discussions with small groups of parents/guardians (up to 10) after the workshop, or if you prefer, you can take part by telephone interview at another time. This is an opportunity to share your views and have your opinion heard.
Please note that you are free to attend the workshop without committing to participate in these additional discussions.
Please read the attached information statement and register your interest if you would like to participate.
Contact the researcher at Conor.Gilligan@newcastle.edu.au or 02 4042 0553 for further information.

original text:
Actually, Printful also falls into the category of dropshipper, or a supplier that stores the products for youpersonally, packages them, and ships them out to your customers after a buy. So, essentially, all you have to do is upload your own layouts and start posting them onto your website to start selling. Within this Printful review, we will outline the benefits of the service and reveal the reason why many users select Printful over the alternatives.
The short story Is that Printful doesn't have as large of selection of products when compared with the competition. If I wanted to publish and dropship some layouts on coasters, I would have to look elsewhere. The same is true for more unique items such as yoga mats and jewelry. However, there is an onslaught of user reviews out there that state Printful is their go-to printing media because of the quality of products. And that's a major thing.
You may have to Cut down on the selection of items that you sell on your website, but you know that the tops are hardy, the printing is not going to wash off quickly, and clients aren't going to get ticked off because of a late delivery.
Of course, not All printing and dropshipping companies are perfect. I've seen users article dreadful Printful reviews, talking about the way the customer support is lacking or the way the print did not appear as smart as they thought. However, nearly all retailers seem to agree that Printful is your leader in this market.
1 thing to Remember is that it often is dependent upon the sort of product you're printing on, the provider you choose, and the quality of graphic you upload. So, 1 type of shirt might look fantastic from Printful, however the tote bags might not look exactly perfect. The primary way to work out if Printful is to you is to upload a few of your layouts and purchase samples. In this manner, you can compare pricing, quality, and shipping rate to other printing companies and see which one is for you.
For This Printful review, we'll prevent the caliber of its products, because that really requires you to purchase some samples. But, we'll talk about pricing, ease-of-use, client support, and the selection of goods. This way, you'll have a better idea of just how Printful can work for you and when the integration and design tools are enough for your industry.
Printful provides the goods, Prints on the products, packages and fulfills the order and sends it straight to your clients, all you have to do is send orders to them and also the sequence information and they will handle the rest. To learn more about how their process works, have a look at the Printful Printing Order Lifecycle.
They are in the business Of assisting you to create products for your business, and they supply all the necessary tools and resources to help you easily integrate the dropshipping process into your ecommerce site so you can offer a seamless experience to your clients. They offer plenty of ecommerce integrations so you can use their service no matter which platform you decide to market, they're transparent about their goods and delivery pricing so it is easy to understand their prices and employ it to your product markups, they supply design, photography and branding services so your products can appear their best and they are located in North America as well as Europe in order to readily reach the largest consumer markets.
Another cost to Consider is your sample sequence. As I mentioned before, the only way to really see if the quality of the products from Printful are perfect for you is to send a sample on your own.
The sample Orders from Printful are fair. You receive a 20% discount on the list cost, together with free shipping to choose locations.
This might cause Problems for those attempting to try out new products on a regular basis. However, the firm does have any ways to gain additional sample orders–such as by integrating with an ecommerce platform or creating more than $300 in the previous month.
Another big Question most retailers have is whether or not it's fast and easy to walk through the print layout centre. Also, what's it like trying to get those products on your website?
I'd choose The size which I'd like to sell, then upload the printing document that is going to move onto that canvas.
In general, the Guide from Printful is very clear about what you might want to change with your upload. For instance, most random JPGs you upload in the computer are going to be too small for printing onto a canvas. You are going to want to have a massive JPG or PNG that you exported from a RAW file in something like Lightroom or Photoshop.
After that, you Either place your customized order or store it to your connected ecommerce platform. If you do have it connected to a stage the product automatically syncs to your shop.
Printful Is for ecommerce entrepreneurs who want the ease of selling dropshipped goods without sacrificing their branding in the process. Whether you're trying to find a way to market your designs to your customers be it on custom t-shirts, canvases, phone cases, pillows and much more, or if you're interested in starting any kind of product-based business without the annoyance of owning inventory or manufacturing materials, then Printful is for you.
They are always Growing their products and services offerings, and since they're so receptive to their own customer's needs and wants their number of vendors continues to grow. Their efficiency, transparency, high quality merchandise offering and diverse selection are part of the reason why they're so powerful, and their ability to work with businesses to help them provide the best service for their clients is what keeps them growing.
You do not Need to have Any previous business experience in regards to working with Printful. The services they offer are available to both experienced entrepreneurs as well as new small business owners, so if you've never started your own business or haven't sourced merchandise from a provider before then provide Printful an attempt! There's no minimum order requirement, no monthly charges, you only pay for orders because your customers make them it's a low-risk way to start your own organization.
What they provide sellers Extends far beyond just their product offering and regular dropshipping services. Those are both a huge part of what makes their service so precious but they also offer other tools and resources that allow sellers to set up their stores more easily, create more beautiful products or just generally help sellers use Printful's support to its fullest extent.
There are several Different printing procedures but those who Printful sticks to are Direct-to-Garment (DTG) printing since it's a price and labor efficient way to print single order things (and the main reason they don't require minimum orders), but they also offer you all-over sublimation printing for some clothing items, screen printing in some cases (for big purchases only) and embroidery for hats and coming shortly for attire.
When it comes to Printing, Printful makes it easy for you. You don't have to think about picking the best printing way of the product you're printing on, every product provided on their site is published in the way that best suits the product, so you don't need to explore different printing methods and their advantages and disadvantages, Printful's got it covered.
For more information Regarding their products and product pricing, check out Printful's Custom Products page. If you are interested in dropshipping a product that you don't see offered on Printful yet, check out their Upcoming Product & Features page to find out if it is coming shortly. They listen for their vendors and they add new products regularly, so if there's a product you need to see just let them know via their New Feature Requests page.
Printful also offers a Warehousing and fulfillment service where they are ready to shop, pick, pack, and ship goods on behalf of merchants — they don't even have to be Printful merchandise! Printful has created a sophisticated community of transport, fulfillment, and logistics options they hire out to retailers who demand access to such resources.
As Printful supplies a Dropshipping service, there's no need for one to handle or manage any of this product or inventory, and you do not manage the packaging, shipping or fulfillment aspects . Printful shops, prints, picks, packs, ships and fulfills every item, so you don't ever have to.
This Is the Reason Why dropshipping Is this attractive business model for entrepreneurs who are starting their first store or do not have a large budget to invest in product inventory or manufacturing tools. It supplies a low-risk way for vendors to supply fantastic products to their clients, and it allows sellers to concentrate on other aspects of their shops such as the layouts, branding, customer service or promotion without needing to worry about the technical side of things like printing goods and shipping them.
That can, of course, Mean that you're paying to get Printful's services and also for providing warehousing, shipping, and satisfaction so as to ensure it is a high-value business version for you. While you do not pay for their support in monthly fees or minimum orders, your profit margins will be affected and will be smaller than if you're fabricating products . This drawback isn't exclusive to Printful, it applies to the dropshipping business model as a whole. It is, nevertheless, an important aspect of dropshipping to keep in mind so you can prepare your margins accordingly.
Printful's seasoned Team can also help you with the design requirements you may want to receive your ecommerce store off the floor. Not only will they help you prepare print documents for Printful products, but they are also able to assist you with any design needs such as redesigning logos, making sublimation print files, color-matching print documents, making minor adjustments to publish documents, creating new graphics, making custom art and much more. Even if you're starting on other platforms like TeeSpring, Society6 or Kickstarter, the Printful design group can assist you with the creation of any designs, graphics or artwork you need.
This is an Perfect service For the ecommerce retailer who has an idea about what designs they would like to publish in their products however aren't certain how to bring it the store owner who would like to outsource the artwork creation so they could free up some time to handle different aspects of building their business. With Printful's dedicated design team you are going to be in great hands, so if this is a service you may need, check out Printful's Design Services page here for more information.
These options help Detract from the impersonal belief that dropshipping can provide and helps to keep your brand consistency from your website to the products your clients receive. To find out more about the branding solutions Printful offers, have a look at this page.
As we have mentioned before What makes Printful such a precious service to use is they're dedicated to not only providing excellent service through their product offering and satisfaction duties, but they also go the extra mile to help vendors utilize Printful to its fullest extent.
· Printing Tips: They supply lots of articles that are easily accessible through their site to help vendors understand the various printing advantages and disadvantages such as DTG print file transparency, cloth details, sublimation printing details, garment printing facts, and information about the best way best to create embroidery files.
· Mockup Generator: Utilize their mockup generator to apply your layouts to their products to get a sense of what the final product will appear to be or to create product pictures on your ecommerce store.
· Gain Calculator: As we've said, the dropshipping business model inherently means you'll be working with slender profit margins, so use their profit calculator to determine just what your margins will be for each product so that you can budget your markup so.
Samples: In order to sample things for yourself or test their goods, they provide discounted product and shipping costs to vendors.
· Blog: Printful's blog is a valuable resource to go to if you are interested in learning more about their merchandise, services, integrations or how to really construct your store how you want it. They're experts on their products and services, so in the event that you want any more information about what they do, then check out their blog posts.
Printful Integrates with all the most widely used ecommerce platforms, and installation for the majority of these platforms is easy and fast. Integrating Printful along with your e commerce shop means you don't have to manually deal with every client's order and submit them to Printful yourself. When you incorporate Printful along with your ecommerce platform every time your customers place an order it automatically gets sent to Printful so that you do not have to manage it at all. This means that orders get sent to Printful immediately and there is no chance of losing the order or making an error on the order details during the transition.
While Printful offers many different services, resources and tools which are beneficial to their sellers, there are still some aspects of the services they are working to improve. Let's dive into the benefits and disadvantages of utilizing Printful as a dropshipping service provider.
Ultimately, Printful Offers the most effective dropshipping service when it comes to apparel, accessories and homeware distributing and printing. They may not have a huge merchandise offering but what they do offer is high quality, and they try to get it right every time. They also work with their sellers to always improve the products and services they provide whether that's getting new types of merchandise in or adding more selection to what they currently offer. Their drive to provide high quality services and products and to constantly improve their products, resources, and tools is the reason so many sellers choose to work with them.
However, the Main ones which also provide dropshipping include Printify (easy to get confused with Printful,) Print Aura, and Gooten. Some different ones exist, however, there aren't many dropshipper/printers in the video game.
So you're Basically registering for a free account, designing your products, and listing them on your site –all for no charge.
Thus, let us say I Wanted to market some t-shirts with my custom layout printed onto them. Printful has a large group of shirts from different brands and made out of different materials. Each product gets the info listed for pricing, colors, shipping places, and reviews.
One of the main Reasons I like Printful so much is because it concentrates on particular goods, but does them well. As an example, t-shirts sound so simple, but a few of the t-shirts are of incredible quality, and that means that you may turn your shop into a bit of a high-end supplier.
I'd argue The Printful base costs are someplace in the middle compared to the competition. I've seen some fairly cheap prices from rivals like Printify and Gooten, but once more, those two choices seem to have more trouble with transport and general quality.
I am convinced You can surely turn a profit for most of your sales through Printful, as long as you are not pricing items over what people would ordinarily pay. People know that habit printing is more expensive, so they're typically ready to pay a premium. In addition, you get to determine the potential gain before publishing it to your website, so if a product does not yield a fantastic return, simply search for a more affordable model or hike up the cost a bit.

original text:
I like Lent. I always have. By a curious Providence, I have always associated it with a period in my life (long before I was Catholic) when I discovered that things like poverty and being out of work and trusting God for your next meal could be refreshing and adventuresome and part of the joy of youth when the world is at your feet and all your road lies before you. The Franciscanism of Lent and this particular time of year (in the northern hemisphere) — when the whole world is waking up again and Spring is peeping up through the thawing soil and the trees are just starting to be edged with green again — is something I love. I love the sharp astringent quality of Lent. I love the fasting and the Back to Basics plainness of it.
You may not even begin to believe this, and it is actually difficult to say but I don't even know what "lent" is.
Lent is the forty day period from Ash Wednesday (March 1 this year) to the Triduum (beginning on Holy Thursday). It is devoted to prayer, fasting. almsgiving, and joining with Jesus in his time in the desert.
Thank you very much Mark Shea. You mean you must fast (that's good), what's aims giving? And Jesus was in the desert then? So he was miserable, probably extremely thirsty, etc., so we should pray for him? Is this majority Catholic, or does it involve most or all religions? I have never been baptised but I'm working on it.
It's always good to ask questions when you don't know something. No shame in that. Almsgiving is the act of sharing what we have with those who need it.
Jesus deliberately went into the desert to be tempted for forty days after his baptism. It was his act of identification both with Israel (which spent forty years in the desert during the Exodus) and with us as sinners in the desert of our own sins. You can read about it in Luke 4:1–13 and Matthew 4:1–11. Mark 1:12-13 mentions it briefly but gives almost no detail.
No, the point is not to pray for Jesus (though that is awfully sweet of you and speaks well of your good heart). Jesus, as Risen Lord and God, does not need our prayers. Rather, it is to pray to him and ask the help of his Spirit as we seek to imitate him in denying ourselves, taking up our cross, and following him.
Lent is a major part of the Christian calendar for the Catholic Church and all the apostolic Churches (those with bishops in historical descent from the apostles). It is also an important season for a number of Protestant Churches. It's only a Christian thing. Other religions also have times of prayer and fasting (Jews observe Yom Kippur, for instance, and Muslims observe Ramadan).
Wow Mark Shea. First thing I want you to know is I don't say things I don't mean. Your msg meant a lot to me. You are an extremely sincere person and you don't see that very often. Also extremely knowledgeable and intelligent. Your message meant a lot me and every word made so much sense. I really "took it to heart", and will never forget what you had to say. Now I'M LOOKING FORWARD TO LENT, all because of your fantastic comment. So, thanks a lot for your message, not only did I learn so much but also it came at the most perfect time. Thank you very much for the book selection.
God love you, dear! May God guide you toward his Son Jesus Christ.
It was a very simple and brief conversation. Thousands of other Catholics could have supplied the same information I did (and have in countless other such conversations with thousands of other inquirers all over the Interwebz or in workplaces, schools or coffee klatches). But it never gets old for me. Watching somebody who just wants to know the truth have an encounter with the gospel and, above all, watching the lights come on as they take it in and make it part of their hearts ... well, there's nothing I find more thrilling and moving. I'm grateful to God for this Providential gift of an encounter with Nancy.
Next articleOur hybrid future? Should we worry about genetically 'humanising' pigs?
processed_text:
I like Lent. I always have. By a curious Providence, I have always associated it with a period in my life (long before I was Catholic) when I discovered that things like poverty and being out of work and trusting God for your next meal could be refreshing and adventuresome and part of the joy of youth when the world is at your feet and all your road lies before you. The Franciscanism of Lent and this particular time of year (in the northern hemisphere)  -  when the whole world is waking up again and Spring is peeping up through the thawing soil and the trees are just starting to be edged with green again  -  is something I love. I love the sharp astringent quality of Lent. I love the fasting and the Back to Basics plainness of it.
You may not even begin to believe this, and it is actually difficult to say but I don't even know what "lent" is.
Lent is the forty day period from Ash Wednesday (March 1 this year) to the Triduum (beginning on Holy Thursday). It is devoted to prayer, fasting. almsgiving, and joining with Jesus in his time in the desert.
Thank you very much Mark Shea. You mean you must fast (that's good), what's aims giving? And Jesus was in the desert then? So he was miserable, probably extremely thirsty, etc., so we should pray for him? Is this majority Catholic, or does it involve most or all religions? I have never been baptised but I'm working on it.
It's always good to ask questions when you don't know something. No shame in that. Almsgiving is the act of sharing what we have with those who need it.
Jesus deliberately went into the desert to be tempted for forty days after his baptism. It was his act of identification both with Israel (which spent forty years in the desert during the Exodus) and with us as sinners in the desert of our own sins. You can read about it in Luke 4:1-13 and Matthew 4:1-11. Mark 1:12-13 mentions it briefly but gives almost no detail.
No, the point is not to pray for Jesus (though that is awfully sweet of you and speaks well of your good heart). Jesus, as Risen Lord and God, does not need our prayers. Rather, it is to pray to him and ask the help of his Spirit as we seek to imitate him in denying ourselves, taking up our cross, and following him.
Lent is a major part of the Christian calendar for the Catholic Church and all the apostolic Churches (those with bishops in historical descent from the apostles). It is also an important season for a number of Protestant Churches. It's only a Christian thing. Other religions also have times of prayer and fasting (Jews observe Yom Kippur, for instance, and Muslims observe Ramadan).
Wow Mark Shea. First thing I want you to know is I don't say things I don't mean. Your msg meant a lot to me. You are an extremely sincere person and you don't see that very often. Also extremely knowledgeable and intelligent. Your message meant a lot me and every word made so much sense. I really "took it to heart", and will never forget what you had to say. Now I'M LOOKING FORWARD TO LENT, all because of your fantastic comment. So, thanks a lot for your message, not only did I learn so much but also it came at the most perfect time. Thank you very much for the book selection.
God love you, dear! May God guide you toward his Son Jesus Christ.
It was a very simple and brief conversation. Thousands of other Catholics could have supplied the same information I did (and have in countless other such conversations with thousands of other inquirers all over the Interwebz or in workplaces, schools or coffee klatches). But it never gets old for me. Watching somebody who just wants to know the truth have an encounter with the gospel and, above all, watching the lights come on as they take it in and make it part of their hearts ... well, there's nothing I find more thrilling and moving. I'm grateful to God for this Providential gift of an encounter with Nancy.
Next articleOur hybrid future? Should we worry about genetically 'humanising' pigs?

original text:
Case packing of cheese blocks | Packing and Sorting Equipment.
Automated cheese packing – The packing process at the Danish dairy, Ullerslev Mejeri, is automated with a new packing machine that handles different types of cheeses and boxes.
The new packing line consists of the packing machine – EGApacker, which handles different types of cheese – round, rectangular and moon-shaped, high or low. These are packed into boxes, with patterns ranging from 1 to 6 cheese blocks in a box. For this purpose, Egatec has developed different grippers, which can easily be replaced depending on the type and number of cheese that are to be placed in boxes. Suction tool is used for the rectangular cheese and different grab tools for the round cheese. The cheese is transported into EGApacker on a conveyor and pushed onto a product plate where the desired layer is formed. Meanwhile, the erected box is released from the buffer position. The gripper grabs the cheese and lowers it into the box.The cardboard magazine, which is integrated inside the packing machine, erects different size of boxes and seals the bottom with tape. The magazine can be adjusted in width and height, so the operator easily can change between the different sizes.A case sealer – EGAsealer, is also included in the new packing line, where the boxes are top sealed with tape before palletizing.The entire line from box erecting, change of gripper to top sealing takes about 5 minutes to reset. For Ullerslev Dairy, Egatec has adapted the standard EGApacker, which has been reversed to fit the production layout.
Ullerslev Dairy is a specialist in cheese packing with a wide range of cheese brands such as "Kong Hans", "Stærke Ullerslev" and "Snehvide". Production started back in 1939 with Danablu and Danbo. Today, 95% of the Danablu is exported.

original text:
Something *closer* this way comes!
You know that feeling, when you're *this* close to something, but it's not here yet, or you don't have it, can't touch it, and yet you feel like you can taste it?
So what do you do? I mean, what do you do, if you're tired of it bugging you and you want something else? You do want to feel better rather than worse, right? Because if you're happy feeling bad, stop reading now because we're not on the same page.
What to do, to feel better and flow with the river already bringing your demonstration to you? How to work with it rather than against it? That is the question... Or 2 questions.
And I say, visualize your goal already done. Of course, you've already done that at some point, or you wouldn't have gotten this far! But this is a journey, and journeys have steps. And now, it's more important than ever to see the thing completed.
Why? Because the alternative, which you may be feeling now, is frustration, impatience, being all fed up with the blasted goal, and what possessed me to want this in the first place? That's also the reason some people say, or people unfortunately say about others, they never finish anything. That can't possibly be true! But we say it, and worse, we believe it. When really, all that's happening is that we lost focus of the thing already done.
Which do you want? You want to see the goal accomplished. You know you do. So that's where you need to shift. Do what you did when it was just a dream, because it's still just a dream, and when it's done and you can see it, guess what! It will still be just a dream!
So be happy about it now. Imagine it done. See it. Feel it. Smell it if it has a smell–which it does, even if you can't conjure it up. Imagine what you do now, with this accomplished. Now live your life that way, today.
Yes, I'm saying, visualize the goal. But it's much more than that. Live like you have it. But there's still more. Know in your heart, in your gut, that it's yours. Because the Spirit that gave you the desire is the same Spirit that makes it tangible in your life. For you and for itself.
This means, whatever good you do in seeing the Truth of any situation or any goal accomplished, you're doing it for Christ, for that Spirit within you that wants only to manifest itself more and more! And you, co-creator that you are, are the vehicle that brings forth your unique life.
So do it for you and do it for God/Spirit. Live your life, now! Don't wait for a better time, "because everything will be great when I have ____." Why can't life be great now? It can, and it is, if you allow it to be.
What is your reality? The frustration and lack that comes from the personal ego? Or the Truth and abundance of the Christ-Mind? St. Paul said, "Let the mind be in you that was in Christ." The key word is "let". Let it be so, this day, for this goal, and any day, for everything. And so it is!
This entry was posted in Law of Attraction, Metaphysics, Nature, New Thought, Spiritual articles, Spiritual practices and tagged Inspirational, Life, Spirituality. Bookmark the permalink.
← Random Acts of Kindness – You can Help!

original text:
Westbury, NY – BioIVT, a leading provider of research models and services for drug and diagnostic development, today announced that it has acquired Biological Specialty Corporation (BSC), a supplier of human blood products and components to the life sciences research community for more than 37 years. BSC is headquartered in Colmar, PA, with two additional FDA-registered donor centers in Allentown, PA and Reading, PA.
Emphasizing its commitment to providing the highest quality biospecimens, BSC's donors are all screened according to AABB guidelines, and its research blood products all undergo the complete battery of FDA-required infectious disease state tests.
BioIVT will retain BSC's three locations and all of its employees. Financial details about this transaction were not disclosed.
Biological Specialty Corporation (BSC) has been a supplier of human blood products and components to the research community for more than 37 years. Products including blood, plasmas and sera, as well as white blood cells, and various other human biologicals are collected at our own FDA-registered donor centers. High quality and personal service are a top priority at BSC and we strive to provide exceptional material that is specific to our clients' needs.

BSC is headquartered in Colmar, PA, with two additional FDA-registered donor centers in Allentown, PA and Reading, PA.
Emphasizing its commitment to providing the highest quality biospecimens, BSC's donors are all screened according to AABB guidelines, and its research blood products all undergo the complete battery of FDA-required infectious disease state tests.
BioIVT will retain BSC's three locations and all of its employees. Financial details about this transaction were not disclosed.
Biological Specialty Corporation (BSC) has been a supplier of human blood products and components to the research community for more than 37 years. Products including blood, plasmas and sera, as well as white blood cells, and various other human biologicals are collected at our own FDA-registered donor centers. High quality and personal service are a top priority at BSC and we strive to provide exceptional material that is specific to our clients' needs.

original text:
Sharron Halpert's experience is wide-ranging. Her clients and team members value her work.
Develop and deliver training courses for the NJ building officials discussing topics such as "Grease Duct Wrap for Commercial Kitchen Exhaust" as well as "1705.17- a Discussion of Firestop Special Inspection requirements and what the Inspector is responsible for delivering". Next semester we hope to add two classes to our roster- One will be a class involving an actual burn facility as STI's NJ location and the other will be "I have a Firestop Submittal- Now What?" a class on how to use the firestop submittal to elicit positive change in the industry and on your next project.
Develop and deliver a handful of firestop related training courses for NJ Schools and Grounds: Common Firestop Problems, Firestop Submittals, Firestop Special Inspection, Grease Duct Wrap, Hollow Core Concrete – getting the firestop right.
HLS consulted on this beautiful high-end condominium project to help the new owners undo the problems of the previous contractors. There were an array of issues that were not code compliant and the new owner immediately identified the issues and called for the support of HLS. We had worked together before and they knew we would help reduce their liability and get things moving in the right direction. We helped create the submittals, train the installers and conduct quality control through out the process.
Massive $3.5 billion project with 4 new hotels with 2,200 rooms and 200,000 square feet of convention space, casino, spa, restaurants, and shopping as well as 1000 acres of beach and a Jack Nicklaus golf course.
The largest privately-funded construction project in the history of the United States: 76 acres; approximately 2400 condominiums and 4800 hotel rooms within five high-rise structures. The site also includes restaurants, parking, theater, convention space, high-end shopping and 150,000 square-foot casino.
Sharron worked for the general contractor and started HLS shortly following completion.
Institutional: Preston Memorial Hospital; Banner Thunderbird, Good Samaritan, and John C Lincoln Hospitals; Scottsdale Healthcare; Carl T Hayden VA Medical Center; University Medical Center; and several penitentiaries.
Here are some testimonials that speak to Halpert Life Safety's industry knowledge, ability to improve collaboration between trades, value engineering that doesn't have a negative impact on the project quality, ways that the firm has saved money, improved quality and improved the level of knowledge of the teams she works with.
I was recently involved in a job that involved many complex circumstances dealing with firestop. I was given Sharron's number for third party inspections and was so thankful for that. Sharron is by far the most knowledgeable person I have met with regards to fire safety, specifically firestop. She is very professional and extremely helpful. She held my hand the entire way and was always there to answer questions and help in any way that she could. This is rare for someone working in her capacity as an inspector. Given her knowledge, there were many times that I felt like it was the wrong decision as things had to be redone; but she helped us get through it and correctly, providing the safest conditions possible. I can assure you that I will call her again for my next job, but it will be as a consultant. Sharron is someone that you want on your side and not the opposing side.
One of the best classes I have taken. GREAT JOB! You love your work.
I was impressed with Sharron's technical knowledge, especially for firestop applications that encapsulate the Mechanical Disciplines in particular (Sheetmetal/Ductwork). Her management skills are superior and her ability to work hand-in-hand both with the prime contractor and others is a testament to the communication skills she possesses.
Sharon's promptness led to quick resolutions to many problems. She is very knowledgeable and a good team player.
Sharron was a tremendous asset, addressing the myriad of firestopping issues that arise on such a large and complex project.
Sharron is very talented, dependable, helpful, and knowledgeable. I have attended many of her training sessions, which were very thorough, interesting, and well-planned.
— Michelle Merillat, Manager, Desert Heating & Plumbing, Co.
...Ms. Halpert has been training for my Department for over a year and I can offer nothing but praise. She is the sometimes-rare trainer that has outstanding technical knowledge and an outstanding style of presenting technical material in a very interactive and engaging way.
All of her evaluations from the past year have been exemplary and we've received multiple individual comments that the training she has provided has been exceptional. So much so that she has been contacted by some groups for in-house training above and beyond what we provide through our contract.
...I met Sharron when she was a speaker to our County Fire Prevention Association meeting. The topic was firestopping and at face value, not exactly a thrilling topic. Sharron took a very dry subject and made it into one of the most interesting and thorough presentations I've seen. She is a wealth of knowledge on this topic and is the most passionate person I have ever met when it comes to stopping the spread of fire with sound construction practices.
Sharron Halpert was responsible for reviewing the work of at least 10 different firestop contractors. She improved the level of life safety on this immense project by identifying various deficiencies in the installed firestop systems and reducing the liability of our company by strictly enforcing industry standards and building codes...Sharron helped identify solutions in a timely fashion that often outpaced the installing companies...She was an invaluable member of our QC team.
Sharron is very detail oriented, dedicated to her projects, and has an infectious upbeat personality. Her years of experience were extremely valuable in the success of passing all County and State fire protection inspections and obtaining sign offs for all code compliance matters.
Ensure Team Understanding of Firestop Building Codes, Standards, UL Details, etc.
Sharron is a dedicated individual and takes her work very seriously. She has a strong sense of urgency with everything she does. She is very thorough with her QC inspection, does good follow-up and provides good technical information for those who need it. You can count on her to get something accomplished, regardless of how small or how big the task.
Sharron is an outstanding QA/QC Engineer and was a real asset to me and the project. Her expertise is matched by no one at our company.
Fire Protection Specialist Sharron Halpert showed skills and patience on a project that required the best that we had to offer. Sharron was at the top of her game. The schedule pushed the project. Often there were times when inspections meant stopping the work of other trades, and that was unacceptable. Sharron took the time to walk the areas in advance to ensure we were all on track. She set up meetings with suppliers to ensure that the various Engineering Judgements would apply...Sharron knew how to get the job done. I consider it a privilege to have worked with such a fine professional as her.
I have had the pleasure of working with, and being trained by, Sharron while we both worked in the Quality department during the City Center construction project. Sharron's professionalism, willingness to take on challenges, knowledge of the task at hand, and ability to see it through to completion makes her a role model and leader.
I am having the pleasure of working with Sharron Halpert as the Firestopping Specialist. Her knowledge of firestopping seems endless, with great attention to detail. Sharron has been extremely helpful in providing recommendations and information to both the GC and their subcontractors that ensure that all firestopping is done properly and will meet all requirements. Sharron has also been extremely proactive in highlighting a multitude of items that could create a problem for all involved if not addressed properly. I have learned a significant amount of the most critical life safety system and can only recommend Sharron at the highest level. I know that having Sharron involved with this project will provide the owner with the best possible product and I hope to work with her again in the future.
I worked with Halpert Life Safety on a large resort project for nearly five years. Sharron and her company were in charge of all firestopping issues on the entire 3 million square foot plus project. She performed the following duties on behalf of CCA. Training and overseeing a Chinese workforce, Interfacing with CCA, ownership architect and various subcontractors, Daily inspections of completed work, planning the forward work to be completed, working with the design team for a better answer to issues. In all my 50 plus years in the construction business I have never worked with a more responsive team and having a very successful outcome for a project of this size.
Contact Us Today to see how we can help you reduce liability and save money!

original text:
Quick update.. I just learned via Tech Crunch that facebook has opened up a brand new client library, that makes it possible to embed ajax-nifty facebook applications on any website! Plus, there's no local server-side code needed, so users can simply paste embed code into their site – much like embedding a simple YouTube Video. No other setup required.
So, will this hamper the adoption of Google's OpenSocial? Why would I want to use an Open Social app with (currently) limited reach, when I can simply embed on my myspace profile an app from facebook that all of my friends already use?

Plus, there's no local server-side code needed, so users can simply paste embed code into their site - much like embedding a simple YouTube Video. No other setup required.
So, will this hamper the adoption of Google's OpenSocial? Why would I want to use an Open Social app with (currently) limited reach, when I can simply embed on my myspace profile an app from facebook that all of my friends already use?

original text: Simplicity and elegance combine in the perfect lines of the Centra vanity by the Wyndham Collection®. If cutting-edge contemporary design is your style then the Centra vanity is for you – modern, chic and built to last a lifetime. Available with green glass, pure white man-made stone, ivory marble or white carrera marble counters, with stunning vessel or undermount sink(s) and matching mirror(s). Featuring soft close door hinges, drawer glides, and meticulously finished with brushed chrome hardware. The attention to detail on this beautiful vanity is second to none. original text:
Downers Grove Grade School District 58 is expected to move ahead with the creation of a master facility plan at its Oct. 10 meeting.
DOWNERS GROVE – Downers Grove Grade School District 58 is expected to move ahead with the creation of a master facility plan at its Oct. 10 meeting.
The Board of Education is scheduled to consider a recommendation by Assistant Superintendent for Business Todd Drafall to spend $23,000 to initiate the process with Wight & Co., the district's architect.
The board will meet at 7 p.m. at Downers Grove Village Hall, 801 Burlington Ave.
The district's recently approved strategic plan includes a goal to "create and act upon a master facility plan to update facilities," he said.
Wight has prepared a cost estimate and scope of work for the first two steps of the plan development, Drafall said.
Step one, the vision process, which includes a vision workshop for the district's facility planning council and engagement sessions with staff and community members, will cost $8,000, according to the proposal. The subsequent step, the creation of a comprehensive facility assessment report, has a cost of $15,000, Drafall said.
The facility planning council met for the first time Sept. 25, and it will hold a visioning meeting Oct. 16, said Megan Hewitt, the district's community relations coordinator. The council is comprised of teachers, administrators and community members.
"The process involves engagement of all aspects of the District 58 community," Drafall said in an email. "We will first engage the facility planning council in a visioning process, then we will engage other community members including staff and parents in a similar process to create a new vision of what tomorrow's education will look like in District 58."
Key priorities that will be considered during the creation of a master faciliy plan include enhancing school safety, adding air conditioning and transitioning sixth grade to the middle schools. Class size and enrollment projections also are important considerations, district officials said.
